ops.title: gomonty@v0.0.14 re-dispatched still-pending host calls after a partial FutureSnapshot drain; resolved by removal in cc-context v0.6.0 (gomonty ripped out for a pydantic-monty uv subprocess)

ops.body:
gomonty@v0.0.14 has an unfixed upstream bug: `waitForFutureResults` (dispatch.go:181-198) returns after the FIRST waiter outcome plus a non-blocking drain, dropping still-in-flight results; monty core then re-emits a FutureSnapshot for the unresolved call IDs and gomonty spawns a SECOND `.Wait()` on the same Waiter — so an unmemoized waiter re-executes the host function (a real tool call: `sh()`, MCP, API cost). Reproduced 200/200 under staggered load, up to 10 executions for 4 gathered awaits; on CI it surfaced only once as flaky `TestConcurrentAwaits` peak=5 (2026-07-06). cc-context's fix (commit 8179ac06, shipped v0.5.1): the `waiter` in `internal/codeexec/runtime.go` memoizes via `sync.Once`, so each host call runs exactly once; the hardened test asserts total invocations == n.

Not yet done: file the bug upstream (ewhauser/gomonty; root fix = receive exactly len(currentWaiters) outcomes, or dedupe dispatch per call ID). The user has not authorized filing an external issue. Once a fixed gomonty is pinned, the local memoization can be simplified away — but it is also a legitimate exactly-once guarantee, so removing it is optional.

**Why this matters beyond the flake:** any `ccx exec` script using `asyncio.gather` could double-execute side-effecting host calls before v0.5.1.

Related: [[ccx-exec-monty-notarization]].
